title: Impetigo follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports recording skin or nasal swab context, frequent recurrence, and microbiology results already reviewed; it does not generate a test or antimicrobial change.
investigations.rationale: The microbiology section supports recording already available swab or microbiology results; it does not order a swab or alter an antimicrobial.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording skin or nasal swab context, frequent recurrence, and microbiology results already reviewed; it does not generate a test or antimicrobial change.

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, failure to improve after treatment, alternative-diagnosis concern, serious illness or cellulitis concern, and prior antibiotic exposure.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact reassessment section supports an impetigo follow-up record of interval change, current symptoms, distribution, systemic context, serious-illness concern and previous treatment exposure without confirming infection or changing therapy.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, failure to improve after treatment, alternative-diagnosis concern, serious illness or cellulitis concern, and prior antibiotic exposure.

referral.text: Supports documenting serious illness, widespread disease with immunocompromise, bullous disease in infancy, recurrence, systemic illness, complication risk, and an already made referral or specialist discussion.
referral.rationale: The referral section supports documenting safety-netting and an arranged follow-up or completed specialist discussion when the clinician has made it; no referral is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting serious illness, widespread disease with immunocompromise, bullous disease in infancy, recurrence, systemic illness, complication risk, and an already made referral or specialist discussion.

sources_used.title: Impetigo: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE antimicrobial and referral adaptation

source_limitations: Exact lesion morphology and extent, bullous status, treatment name and adherence, exposure and household context, diagnosis, culture decision, medicine, dose, decolonisation, infection-control advice, referral, and timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
